misses.item: "taxation of earned income in every layer remains governed by Article III.III" — jurisdictional reservation carving tax out of the SCM; no node states it.
"No additional rate is created; attribution feeds the existing pulse mechanics." — distinct prohibition on rate creation; none of the six L239 nodes carry it.
"the central bank's clearing record is the sole sensor" — binding evidentiary rule for dormant-commercial attribution; the dormant-property node stops at "active enterprise is never reached".

"They do not retain the right to actions whose consequences fall on residents of all five rings simultaneously." — autonomy grant plus its limit; xxv-cross-layer-federal-scope states only the scope rationale.
"may strike any post-ratification federal law that subsequent review determines crossed the line" — post-enactment strike power; xxv-vi-no-charter-backdoor covers only pre-gate redirection.

"the Supreme Court may strike it and redirect it to Article XI" for misclassification caught during/after ratification; xxviii-iii-dual-key-classification covers only pre-drafting concurrence and upward reclassification.

"A regulation that passes 80% ratification becomes binding law within the layer, enforced by the AI governance system" — the binding/enforcement clause; node captures only the 80% threshold and unlimited re-petition.

"Temporary presence, seasonal relocation, and coordinated migration do not confer voting rights." — a prohibition; xxviii-ii-residency-voting-eligibility states only the positive six-of-twelve requirement.

"Residents who move into a regulated area are subject to its regulations. Residents who move out are no longer subject." — obligation on in-migrants; geographic-attachment node states persistence, not subjection.

ii-null-exempt-criminal-acts-track-2 marked deontic=prohibition, but its text mandates action: acts are "flagged on the criminal record log (Track 2) immediately regardless of age" — an obligation/procedure.
iii-v-visitation-breach-no-insulation marked kind=prohibition/deontic=standard, but the text is a mandatory trigger: "Any qualifying breach during a visit triggers standard VMSS consequence".

iii-v-voluntary-filing-lookback-24-months marked deontic=prohibition; L216 text is pure obligation — "Assets transferred ... are subject to the same liquidation schedule" with no prohibitory language.

iii-v-prepositioning-lookback-24-months marked deontic=prohibition; the operative clause is "are subject to the same liquidation schedule" (obligation), the prohibition being only the trailing rationale.
xxvii-child-limitation-last-resort-only marked deontic=standard, but "is never deployed under ordinary operation" is a categorical prohibition.
xxii-president-steps-off-ranking marked kind=prohibition while its own deontic and text are obligation ("The President steps off the executive-doctrinal-leadership ranking upon appointment").
iii-vi-sti-recovery-no-upward-restoration marked deontic=standard, but "does not restore eligibility for upward movement" is a prohibition.
x-prior-crimes-counted marked deontic=standard; "Prior crimes are counted" is a mandatory input to the re-entry assessment (obligation/procedure).

criticFindings.summary:
Read charter.html L147-474 in full and enumerated every obligation, prohibition, permission, entitlement, and binding procedure.
Coverage is high but not exact: 9 deontic clauses sit in zero node, concentrated in III.VII savings-base (L234, L239 x2), the XXV.VI/XXVIII.III strike-and-redirect powers (L412, L460), XXVIII.I enforcement (L440), XXVIII.II voting and subjection rules (L445, L449), and the XXV autonomy limit (L373).
8 nodes carry a deontic value that contradicts their own text: obligations labelled prohibition (L175, L206, L216), prohibitions labelled standard (L220, L225, L426), one obligation labelled standard (L277), one kind/deontic conflict (L331).
24 duplicate clusters cover the same rule unit twice or more. Most are canonical restatements the extraction mirrored rather than deduplicated: Article V L254-256 vs Article VIII L271-273 (six pairs), Article I L160-164 vs Articles VI and XV, the III.IV/III.V/III.VI vs VII mobility-and-transfer overlaps, XI L293-294 vs XXI L325 and XXV.VI L400-409, and XII L298 vs XIII L300.
One hard defect: node id `xi-pillar-article-marker` is emitted twice (L278-279, L278-280) — a literal id collision, not a semantic duplicate.
Quotes are byte-faithful to the source; canon hedges preserved.
Read-only run; no files modified, no git state touched.

misses.item: "+1 Sanctuary votes by consensus — not a supermajority, full agreement" — the strictest vote threshold in the Charter has no ledger row anywhere (also restated 289, 295, and quantified at 404 as "Relaxed from XI's full consensus by ten points", where only "ten points" is owned).
"No minimum participation quorum is imposed." — an operative quorum magnitude (quorum = none) on the Article XI ratification vote.
"There is no minimum wage in VMSS." — operative wage-floor magnitude (floor = none); the line's other 11 literals are all owned by overtime nodes.
"Violations of federal law in lower layers are addressed through a two-track escalation system." — count literal; ledger jumps from 379 to 387 and 388.

"Classification of a petition as regulatory or structural is dual-key: both the Meritboard's ... and the Supreme Court must concur" — two-body concurrence threshold plus either-body upward reclassification; no node covers line 460.
"suspension is one-case, time-limited, and automatically expires when the emergency resolves" — bounded-scope magnitude on Supreme Court charter suspension; no node covers line 293.

"No single metric, including STI, shall unilaterally determine punitive layer assignment or descent into lower layers." — Article XII has zero nodes; same literal class as the ledgered "single measurable criterion" (275) and "single-axis" (302).
"System decisions that produce punitive layer reassignment must remain structurally independent from any single metric." — Article XIII has zero nodes.
"a single body — including the Chief Architect — cannot unilaterally rewrite the terms under which every resident lives." — count constraint; same class as the ledgered "two branches" (332).
"There is no limit on consecutive terms" — term-limit magnitude (none); the two ten-year literals on the same line are owned, this one is not.

criticFindings.summary:
Re-derived charter.html:147-474 independently. The numeric inventory (currency, percent, ratio, fraction, multiplier, trajectory rows, dates) matches the ledger exactly — every $, %, x, n/n and n:n literal I enumerated has an owner, including all 12 leakage rows and both 24-month lookbacks.
All 10 misses are non-numeric or negative-magnitude literals: vote thresholds expressed in words (consensus), floors expressed as absences (no minimum wage, no quorum, no term limit, one-case), and count literals (two-track, dual-key, single metric).
Articles XII (298) and XIII (300) have zero nodes in the ledger despite carrying "single metric" literals of the same class the ledger owns at 275 and 302. Lines 291, 293 and 460 are likewise entirely uncovered.
Double-classification is concentrated in cross-article restatement. xi-federal-mirror-relaxed-thresholds (294) is the worst: it owns all five XXV.VI thresholds a second time and bundles five rule units into one node.
The ledger already has a correct pattern for this — xxvi-sti-mechanics-cross-reference (419) declares its 10:1 as a cross-reference to 167. The 294, 242, 289 and 175 restatements do not, so they read as independent owners.
xxiii-generational-tightening (361) classifies rhetorical restatement of the 2150/3000 trajectory endpoints already owned by the schedule node — over-classification rather than a gap.
Not reported (judged in-clause, not skips): "single qualifying event" (164), "no floor, no exemptions, no means testing" (230), the 60–90% context restatement (423), monthly/annual cadences at 228/446.
